author: codex
scope: validator/rule-data triage only for next relation endpoint bucket after next6/relation025; artifact-only prep; no provider-backed post-ingest commands; no generated-output, override, journal, manifest, LOG, wiki, or code edits

recommendation.summary: Do not widen has_timeline_event to accept metric_claim subjects. After excluding the already-addressed next4 has_metric claim patch plus relation023, relation024, and relation025 cleanup originals, the next top endpoint-shape bucket is four has_timeline_event metric_claim -> event rows. The examples use metric claims as timeline carriers for delivery dates, a presentation date, or a board approval date. Metrics are not durable timeline owners; the evidence supports loose same-context associations or event/source-owned chronology, not metric-owned chronology.
recommendation.recommended_action: Prepare a narrow relation cleanup batch for the four malformed has_timeline_event metric_claim -> event relations. Retire the original submissions and replace row-by-row only where a source-backed non-timeline relation is useful. Prefer unshaped dependency/reference relations such as related_to or supports for weak contextual links; do not add metric_claim as an allowed has_timeline_event subject.

why_not_validator_patch.reason: has_timeline_event is intended for project, route, corridor, entity, source, and event timeline carriers. The four inspected rows do not show metrics owning lifecycle events. Widening the validator would hide extraction/modeling errors and would be inconsistent with the next6 claim-owned timeline cleanup decision.

excluded_prior_actions: next4 has_metric claim -> metric_claim validator patch
relation023 has_timeline_event treatment_component -> event cleanup originals
relation024 implemented_by treatment_component -> entity cleanup originals
relation025 has_timeline_event claim -> event cleanup originals
checkpoint_context.current_code_context: packages/pipeline/src/records/relations.ts currently accepts has_metric claim -> metric_claim, has_metric event -> metric_claim, has_claim event -> claim, has_timeline_event source -> event, and has_timeline_event event -> event. It still rejects has_timeline_event metric_claim -> event.
checkpoint_context.method: Read-only bun --eval over readCanonicalRecords() and relationEndpointShapeIssue(); excluded relation023, relation024, and relation025 original record ids from their prep artifacts; did not run CLI validate, materialize, export-jsonl, DB repair/publish, or provider-backed commands.

top_shape_buckets.classification: defer; likely agency-role direction or relation-kind cleanup, not a validator widening
defer; likely agency-role direction or relation-kind cleanup, not a validator widening
defer; publication role should attach source, project, or entity carriers, not claim subjects
defer; source object suggests containment/reference cleanup
defer; likely endpoint identity or relation-kind cleanup

simulated_impact.assumption: All four original malformed relations are retired and any replacements use validator-compatible or unshaped relation kinds. This artifact did not create replacement submissions, edit retired.json, edit submissions journals, or run createSubmissionEntry dry-runs.
simulated_impact.why_cleanup_has_same_endpoint_count_impa: A validator patch admitting has_timeline_event metric_claim -> event would also remove four endpoint-shape issues, but it would validate metric-owned chronology. Cleanup preserves the validator signal.

object.display_name: Delivery Start of Base Buses - April 2025

evidence.text: NYC Transit requests that the Board waive formal competitive bidding and approve the purchase of 250 over-the-road express diesel buses ... in the total estimated amount of $325,795,912 ($214,187,493 for the base award and $111,608,420 for the options) on behalf of MTA Bus Company.
Delivery of the base buses is anticipated to begin in April 2025 and be completed by August 2026.

exact_issue_examples.triage: The amount and delivery-start event are same-procurement context, but the amount is not a timeline carrier. If preserving the association is useful, use a loose related_to/supports replacement rather than has_timeline_event.

object.display_name: Delivery Completion of Base Buses - August 2026

evidence.text: NYC Transit requests that the Board waive formal competitive bidding and approve the purchase of 250 over-the-road express diesel buses ... in the total estimated amount of $325,795,912 ($214,187,493 for the base award and $111,608,420 for the options) on behalf of MTA Bus Company.
Delivery of the base buses is anticipated to begin in April 2025 and be completed by August 2026.

exact_issue_examples.triage: Same pattern as the delivery-start row: the evidence supports a procurement schedule, not metric-owned chronology.

subject.display_name: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 deferral impact $5B

object.display_name: Presentation to MTA Board on June 26, 2024

evidence.text: Future Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 contracts to be deferred or shifted to the 2025-2029 Capital Plan.

exact_issue_examples.triage: The metric belongs to the presentation/source context. The presentation date should not be modeled as a timeline event of the metric claim.

evidence.text: Board Action Order To Date Approval Info Other 1 MTA B&T Committee 02/26/24 X 2 MTA Finance Committee 02/26/24 X 3 MTA Board 02/28/24 X

exact_issue_examples.triage: The board event is approval context for the surplus action. It may support or relate to the metric claim, but the operating surplus metric should not own the board-meeting timeline.

guards_for_followup_cleanup_prep: Verify each original submission id is still present and not already retired before preparing a cleanup batch.
Check for existing same-source replacement relations to avoid duplicate related_to/supports rows.
Prefer dropping a relation over adding a weak replacement when the only support is co-occurrence in a table or agenda section.
If a stronger carrier exists in the same source, prefer event/source/project-owned chronology rather than metric_claim-owned chronology. Do not infer or create project records in this triage lane.

commands_run.outcome: Confirmed repo root and existing next/cleanup prep artifacts through next6 and relation025.
Observed a heavily dirty worktree from other lanes; no unrelated changes were reverted or overwritten.
Skimmed current log context; confirmed next4, relation023, relation024, and relation025 status and the generated-output restrictions while wave25 remains live.
